summary: SPY is 8.2% above its 200-day average (bullish structural trend), but the market is trading at elevated valuations and faces two near-term binary events: reciprocal tariffs could snap back to higher rates on July 9 (3 days away) unless extended, and June CPI releases on July 14. The Fed holds at 3.5-3.75% with a hawkish posture and no cuts expected. June jobs came in well below expectations (57K vs 115K). All three watchlist names with active theses are priced above our fair-value estimates. Demand a wider margin of safety before buying.
events: July 9 (Thu): Reciprocal tariff rates revert to higher levels unless extended — binary event, 3 days away, unresolved at market open
July 14 (Tue): June CPI release at 8:30 AM ET — key inflation reading; May ran at 4.2% YoY
July 24 (Fri): Section 122 tariffs (10% global import duty) expire
July 28-29 (Mon-Tue): FOMC meeting, rate decision July 29 at 2:00 PM ET — 89% probability of hold at 3.5-3.75%
rates: steady at 3.5-3.75%, hawkish hold, no cuts expected at July 28-29 meeting
inflation: May CPI 4.2% YoY; June CPI data due July 14
valuation: forward P/E ~20.4 (above 5Y avg 19.9 and 10Y avg 19.0); trailing P/E ~25.4; CAPE ~39.9 (125% above historical mean)
futures_am: S&P 500 futures up ~0.45% at Monday morning open; positive momentum after July 4 holiday weekend
key_risks: July 9 tariff cliff: reciprocal tariffs snap back to higher rates unless the administration grants another extension — outcome unknown as of this morning
CPI July 14: inflation running at 4.2% YoY in May; another hot print would reinforce the hawkish Fed hold and pressure multiples
Fed hawkish hold: no rate relief imminent; new Chair Kevin Warsh signaled reduced forward guidance, adding policy uncertainty
Geopolitical: Middle East conflict and Iran have disrupted oil markets; China restricting rare-earth exports to strategic adversaries
Labor softening: June jobs +57K well below expectations; April and May also revised down a combined 74K
key_tailwinds: SPY is 8.2% above its 200-day SMA — structural uptrend intact, not a broken market
S&P 500 futures up ~0.45% at open; positive post-holiday momentum, Dow recently hit fresh record high
Oil prices down ~30% from May peak (~$69/bbl) — easing energy inflation pressure
Q2 2026 earnings growth tracking ~23% YoY — corporate fundamentals still solid despite macro headwinds
note: All three watchlist names with active theses are above our base fair values. AAPL is 60% above fair value, V is 21% above, MSFT is essentially at fair value. No name clears our margin-of-safety threshold today. Cautious posture means we hold cash and require a bigger-than-normal discount before acting.
